error.signature: OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device in AWS Lambda /tmp

verdict.summary: Lambda /tmp is limited to 512MB by default (expandable to 10GB since 2022). But /tmp persists across warm invocations! Previous invocation's temp files eat into the next invocation's quota.

dead_ends.action: Increase Lambda memory to get more /tmp space
dead_ends.why_fails: Lambda memory and /tmp storage are independent. More memory does NOT increase /tmp. You must configure ephemeral storage separately.

dead_ends.action: Write temp files and assume they're cleaned up between invocations
dead_ends.why_fails: /tmp is NOT cleaned between warm invocations. Files from previous invocations persist and accumulate.

workarounds.action: Clean /tmp at the start of each invocation
workarounds.how: import shutil, os; [os.remove(os.path.join('/tmp', f)) for f in os.listdir('/tmp')]

workarounds.action: Configure ephemeral storage up to 10GB in Lambda config
Use S3 or EFS for large file processing instead of /tmp
workarounds.how: Mount EFS filesystem or stream data to/from S3 instead of downloading to /tmp
